1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_abjure}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation formally renounces an
5	{rbtga_ark}
6	from
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	using the Docker Registry HTTP API V2 implemented by
9	{gar_service}.
10	
11	An
12	{rbtga_ark}
13	is the coherent unit of a Recipe Bottle build: a set of artifacts sharing a common
14	{rbtga_hallmark}
15	but distinguished by suffix.
16	The
17	{rbtga_ark_image}
18	(`-image`) contains the container image itself.
19	The
20	{rbtga_ark_about}
21	(`-about`) contains SBOM and build metadata.
22	The
23	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
24	(`-vouch`) contains verification results.
25	The
26	{rbtga_ark_pouch}
27	(`-pouch`) contains the build context (conjure/bind only, absent for graft).
28	A fifth artifact, `-diags`, is present for conjure arks only (build-time diagnostics forwarded to the about pipeline).
29	Abjuring an ark removes all existing artifacts as a single logical operation.
30	
31	The
32	{rbtr_director}
33	abjures an ark:
34	
35	//axhopt_typed_parameter
36	{rbtga_ark}
37	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
38	.. {rbbc_require}
39	`«INPUT_VESSEL»` provided as first argument
40	.. {rbbc_require}
41	`«INPUT_HALLMARK»` provided as second argument
42	.. {rbbc_fatal}
43	if vessel missing: "Vessel parameter required"
44	.. {rbbc_fatal}
45	if hallmark missing: "Hallmark parameter required"
46	
47	//axhopt_typed_parameter
48	{rbtga_hallmark}
49	. *Validate Hallmark Format*:
50	.. {rbbc_require}
51	`«INPUT_HALLMARK»` matches pattern `[cbg]\d{12}-r\d{12}` (mode prefix + dual timestamp)
52	.. {rbbc_fatal}
53	if malformed: "Hallmark format invalid"
54	
55	//axhos_step
56	. *Authenticate as
57	{rbtr_director}*:
58	.. Execute
59	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
60	.. {rbbc_store}
61	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
62	
63	//axhos_step
64	. *Construct Registry Endpoint*:
65	.. {rbbc_store}
66	`«REGISTRY_HOST»` as `{rbrr_gcp_region}-docker.pkg.dev`
67	.. {rbbc_store}
68	`«REGISTRY_PATH»` as `{rbrr_depot_project_id}/{rbrr_gar_repository}`
69	.. {rbbc_store}
70	`«REGISTRY_BASE»` as `https://«REGISTRY_HOST»/v2/«REGISTRY_PATH»`
71	
72	//axhos_step
73	. *Construct Ark Tags*:
74	.. {rbbc_store}
75	`«IMAGE_TAG»` as `«INPUT_VESSEL»:«INPUT_HALLMARK»-image`
76	.. {rbbc_store}
77	`«ABOUT_TAG»` as `«INPUT_VESSEL»:«INPUT_HALLMARK»-about`
78	.. {rbbc_store}
79	`«VOUCH_TAG»` as `«INPUT_VESSEL»:«INPUT_HALLMARK»-vouch`
80	.. {rbbc_store}
81	`«POUCH_TAG»` as `«INPUT_VESSEL»:«INPUT_HALLMARK»-pouch`
82	.. {rbbc_store}
83	`«DIAGS_TAG»` as `«INPUT_VESSEL»:«INPUT_HALLMARK»-diags`
84	.. If vessel mode is conjure:
85	... For each platform in `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS`:
86	.... Derive `«ARCH_SUFFIX»` from platform (e.g., `linux/amd64` → `amd64`, `linux/arm/v7` → `armv7`)
87	.... {rbbc_store}
88	`«ATTEST_TAG_n»` as `«INPUT_VESSEL»:«INPUT_HALLMARK»-attest-«ARCH_SUFFIX»`
89	
90	//axhos_step
91	. *Verify Ark Existence*:
92	.. {rbbc_call}
93	link:https://github.com/opencontainers/distribution-spec/blob/main/spec.md#pulling-manifests[Docker Registry API: HEAD manifests] for `«IMAGE_TAG»`:
94	... method: `HEAD`
95	... endpoint: `«REGISTRY_BASE»/«INPUT_VESSEL»/manifests/«INPUT_HALLMARK»-image`
96	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
97	... header: `Accept: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json, application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json, application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json, application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json`
98	.. {rbbc_store}
99	`«IMAGE_EXISTS»` as true if HTTP 200, false if HTTP 404
100	.. {rbbc_call}
101	same HEAD request for `«ABOUT_TAG»`:
102	... endpoint: `«REGISTRY_BASE»/«INPUT_VESSEL»/manifests/«INPUT_HALLMARK»-about`
103	.. {rbbc_store}
104	`«ABOUT_EXISTS»` as true if HTTP 200, false if HTTP 404
105	.. {rbbc_call}
106	same HEAD request for `«VOUCH_TAG»`:
107	... endpoint: `«REGISTRY_BASE»/«INPUT_VESSEL»/manifests/«INPUT_HALLMARK»-vouch`
108	.. {rbbc_store}
109	`«VOUCH_EXISTS»` as true if HTTP 200, false if HTTP 404
110	.. {rbbc_call}
111	same HEAD request for `«POUCH_TAG»`:
112	... endpoint: `«REGISTRY_BASE»/«INPUT_VESSEL»/manifests/«INPUT_HALLMARK»-pouch`
113	.. {rbbc_store}
114	`«POUCH_EXISTS»` as true if HTTP 200, false if HTTP 404 (optional — conjure/bind only, absent for graft)
115	.. {rbbc_call}
116	same HEAD request for `«DIAGS_TAG»`:
117	... endpoint: `«REGISTRY_BASE»/«INPUT_VESSEL»/manifests/«INPUT_HALLMARK»-diags`
118	.. {rbbc_store}
119	`«DIAGS_EXISTS»` as true if HTTP 200, false if HTTP 404 (optional — conjure-only, absent for bind/graft)
120	.. If `«ATTEST_TAG_n»` tags were constructed (conjure vessels):
121	... For each `«ATTEST_TAG_n»`:
122	.... {rbbc_call}
123	same HEAD request:
124	..... endpoint: `«REGISTRY_BASE»/«INPUT_VESSEL»/manifests/«INPUT_HALLMARK»-attest-«ARCH_SUFFIX»`
125	.... {rbbc_store}
126	`«ATTEST_n_EXISTS»` as true if HTTP 200, false if HTTP 404
127	
128	//axhos_step
129	. *Evaluate Ark State*:
130	.. If all of `«IMAGE_EXISTS»`, `«ABOUT_EXISTS»`, and `«VOUCH_EXISTS»` are false:
131	... {rbbc_fatal}
132	"Ark not found: no artifacts exist"
133	.. If exactly one exists:
134	... {rbbc_warn}
135	"Orphaned artifact detected"
136	... {rbbc_show}
137	which artifact exists and which is missing
138	.. {rbbc_store}
139	`«ARTIFACTS_TO_DELETE»` as list of existing artifacts
140	
141	//axhos_step
142	. *Confirm Abjuration*:
143	.. Unless `--force` flag provided:
144	... {rbbc_show}
145	"Will abjure ark «INPUT_VESSEL»/«INPUT_HALLMARK»:" followed by each artifact in `«ARTIFACTS_TO_DELETE»`
146	... {rbbc_require}
147	user types `yes` to confirm
148	... {rbbc_fatal}
149	if not confirmed: "prompt not confirmed."
150	
151	//axhos_step
152	. *Delete Image Artifact* (if exists):
153	.. If `«IMAGE_EXISTS»`:
154	... {rbbc_call}
155	link:https://github.com/opencontainers/distribution-spec/blob/main/spec.md#deleting-tags[Docker Registry API: DELETE manifests] with:
156	.... method: `DELETE`
157	.... endpoint: `«REGISTRY_BASE»/«INPUT_VESSEL»/manifests/«INPUT_HALLMARK»-image`
158	.... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
159	... {rbbc_require}
160	HTTP 202 (Accepted) or 204 (No Content) response
161	... {rbbc_fatal}
162	if delete fails: "Failed to delete -image artifact (HTTP «STATUS_CODE»)"
163	
164	//axhos_step

file.content:
rbfl_abjure() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
  local z_force="${3:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Abjure a hallmark — delete all GAR packages under hallmarks/<hallmark>/"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path (retained for AAK signature; drops in AAL)"
  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
  buc_doc_param "--force" "Optional: skip confirmation prompt"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Vessel resolution kept for AAK signature compatibility — vessel no longer
  # appears in the registry path, but the load still validates the moniker.
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"

  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark parameter required"

  local z_skip_confirm=false
  if test "${z_force}" = "--force"; then
    z_skip_confirm=true
  fi

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  # Enumerate packages under hallmarks/<hallmark>/ via GAR REST API.
  # Each immediate child of the subtree is one ark (image, vouch, pouch,
  # about, attest, diags). Iterating discovered children rather than a
  # hardcoded suffix list naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch.
  local -r z_subtree="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/"
  buc_step "Enumerating packages under ${z_subtree}"

  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_abjure_list"

  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List packages for abjure" "${z_list_infix}"

  # GAR returns package names URL-encoded in the resource name (slashes as
  # %2F); decode and prefix-match to the hallmark subtree.
  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"
  local -r z_pkg_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}packages.txt"

  jq -r --arg subtree "${z_subtree}" '
    .packages[]?.name
    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
    | select(startswith($subtree))
  ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_pkg_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract package names for hallmark subtree"

  if ! test -s "${z_pkg_file}"; then
    buc_die "No packages found under ${z_subtree} — hallmark not present in registry"
  fi

  local z_count
  z_count=$(wc -l < "${z_pkg_file}" | tr -d ' ')

  # Confirm abjuration unless --force
  if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"; then
    local z_confirm_msg="Will abjure ${z_count} packages under ${z_subtree}:"
    local z_pkg_path=""
    while IFS= read -r z_pkg_path || test -n "${z_pkg_path}"; do
      z_confirm_msg="${z_confirm_msg}\n  - ${z_pkg_path}"
    done < "${z_pkg_file}"
    buc_require "${z_confirm_msg}" "yes"
  fi

  # Delete each package via GAR REST API.
  # DELETE returns a long-running operation; trust 200 as accepted (matches
  # the prior fire-and-forget semantics with 202/204 on the v2 manifest API).
  local z_pkg_path=""
  local z_del_idx=0
  while IFS= read -r z_pkg_path || test -n "${z_pkg_path}"; do
    buc_step "Deleting package: ${z_pkg_path}"

    local z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg_path//\//%2F}"
    local z_del_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}"
    local z_del_infix="rbfl_abjure_del_${z_del_idx}"

    rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_del_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_del_infix}"
    rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete package ${z_pkg_path}" "${z_del_infix}"

    buc_info "Deleted: ${z_pkg_path}"
    z_del_idx=$((z_del_idx + 1))
  done < "${z_pkg_file}"

  echo ""
  buc_success "Hallmark abjured: ${z_hallmark} (${z_count} packages)"
}

rbfl_tally() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

content.text: Now dispatching five agents in parallel. Each gets the same binding conversion rules.

input.description: Agent A — RBS0, RBSAA, RBSAV spec rewrites
input.prompt:
You are rewriting AsciiDoc spec files for the Recipe Bottle project. This is Notch 3 of ₢A_AAK — the GAR categorical-layout migration. The code has already been migrated; spec files still describe the old layout and must be updated to match the new one. Your goal is **grep-zero**: after your work, no stale patterns remain in your files.

## Binding path shapes (post-migration reality, do not deviate)

```
Arks:        ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/<hallmark>/<basename>:<hallmark>
Reliquaries: ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/<date>/<tool>:<date>
Enshrines:   ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/<anchor>:<anchor>
```

Root constants (RBGL_*) kindle from `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}<category>`. Category literals: `"hallmarks"`, `"reliquaries"`, `"enshrines"`. Ark basenames: `image`, `about`, `vouch`, `pouch`, `diags`, `attest`. Attest is a **single package with per-platform tags**: `.../attest:<hallmark>-<ARCH>`.

## Binding vocabulary conversions

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-image` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/image:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-about` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-vouch` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/vouch:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-pouch` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/pouch:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-diags` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/diags:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-attest-<ARCH>` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/attest:<hallmark>-<ARCH>` |
| `{repo}/enshrine:{anchor}` | `<prefix>enshrines/<anchor>:<anchor>` |
| `vouches:<hallmark>-vouch` (the aggregator) | DISSOLVED — hallmark-only lookup via subtree navigation |
| "vouches superdirectory/package/aggregator" phrase | rephrase as "hallmark subtree" or drop as redundant |
| `RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_*` constant | `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_*` |
| `_RBGA_ARK_SUFFIX_*` attribute ref | `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_*` |
| `_RBGV_ARK_SUFFIX_*` attribute ref | `_RBGV_ARK_BASENAME_*` |
| `_RBGY_ARK_SUFFIX_*` attribute ref | `_RBGY_ARK_BASENAME_*` |
| `RBGC_VOUCHES_PACKAGE` | DELETED (the constant is gone — delete any attribute or reference) |
| `_RBGV_VOUCHES_PACKAGE` attribute | DELETE the row entirely |

When a spec attribute refers to a deleted constant, delete the attribute row from the mapping table. When prose describes "hyphen-suffix tag", "suffix", "tagged as `-image`", etc., rewrite the narrative to match the basename/subtree shape.

## Your files (3 total)

1. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`** — the project's top-level spec. Heaviest file, 40+ hits. Has a dense attestation section (lines ~1690–1990) referencing `-image/-attest-<ARCH>/-about/-vouch/-pouch/-diags` repeatedly, plus a "vouches superdirectory" explanation (~line 1716–1721) that needs rewording because the aggregator no longer exists.

2. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAA-ark_abjure.adoc`** — spec for the abjure operation. The algorithm itself has been rewritten in code; prose must match. Current spec describes a per-suffix HEAD/DELETE dance; new spec describes enumerate-then-delete via the **GAR REST API packages endpoint** (NOT the Docker Registry V2 manifests API anymore). See "Abjure rewrite" below for the exact new flow.

3. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAV-ark_vouch.adoc`** — spec for vouch. The dual-tag pattern (`push to both VESSEL:HALLMARK-vouch and vouches:HALLMARK-vouch`) dissolves — vouch is now a single package under `hallmarks/<hallmark>/vouch`. The `_RBGV_VOUCHES_PACKAGE` attribute row must be deleted. Prose explaining "vouches superdirectory enables hallmark-only lookup" must be reworded: subtree navigation gives hallmark-only lookup for free.

## Abjure rewrite specifics (RBSAA)

The actual implementation (`Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:264–359`) does this:

1. Authenticate as Director (same as before)
2. **NEW**: Use GAR REST API `GET /packages?pageSize=1000` to list all packages in the repository (not Docker Registry V2 manifests API)
3. Filter package names that start with `hallmarks/<hallmark>/` (URL-encoded with `%2F`)
4. If zero matches → "hallmark not present in registry" fatal
5. Confirm with user (unless `--force`) — show the count and list of packages to be deleted
6. For each filtered package → GAR REST API `DELETE /packages/<URL-encoded-name>`
7. Report count abjured

Key shifts to capture in prose:
- API family: GAR REST API (`/packages`) instead of Docker Registry HTTP API V2 (`/manifests`)
- Unit of delete: a **package**, not a manifest tag (deleting a package removes all its tags)
- No separate HEAD-then-DELETE: the enumerate step IS the existence check
- No orphan detection: if it's in the subtree, it gets deleted; no "orphaned artifact detected" warning anymore
- Existing asciidoc structure (`//axhos_step`, `{rbbc_*}` helpers, `.. {rbbc_call}` syntax) stays — just the content of each step changes
- The original "Construct Ark Tags" step (lines 72–88) collapses since tags aren't pre-constructed; enumeration discovers them
- The individual "Delete X Artifact" steps (lines 151–227) collapse into one "Delete Each Enumerated Package" step with a loop

Note: vessel parameter is still accepted by the operation (AAK signature compatibility, drops in AAL) but is not used to construct the subtree path. Mention this retention briefly.

## How to work

1. Read each file first. Understand the existing prose structure and tone.
2. Edit in place using the Edit tool. Preserve the asciidoc style: attribute refs `{rbtgo_*}`, anchors, includes, `//axho*` section markers, bullet structure.
3. Don't add comments explaining changes. Don't add emojis.
4. For RBSAA, the algorithm section (roughly lines 64–227) needs substantial restructuring — not just word substitution. Feel free to rewrite whole steps where the old flow no longer applies.
5. For RBS0, hit every occurrence — the attestation section is dense; be thorough.
6. For RBSAV, delete `_RBGV_VOUCHES_PACKAGE` attribute row entirely (don't leave a stub).

## When complete

Report back (under 300 words):
- Files edited, with approximate hit count cleaned per file
- Any structural restructuring you did on RBSAA (which steps you collapsed/rewrote)
- Any surprising findings or inconsistencies in the original prose you had to resolve
- Any remaining concerns — especially anywhere you were unsure about the new shape

**Do not use `git` commands. Do not commit. Do not run tests. Edit files only.**

input.description: Agent B — RBSAB, RBSAC, RBSAE, RBSAS spec rewrites
input.prompt:
You are rewriting AsciiDoc spec files for the Recipe Bottle project. This is Notch 3 of ₢A_AAK — the GAR categorical-layout migration. The code has already been migrated; spec files still describe the old layout and must be updated to match the new one. Your goal is **grep-zero**: after your work, no stale patterns remain in your files.

## Binding path shapes (post-migration reality, do not deviate)

```
Arks:        ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/<hallmark>/<basename>:<hallmark>
Reliquaries: ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/<date>/<tool>:<date>
Enshrines:   ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/<anchor>:<anchor>
```

Root constants (RBGL_*) kindle from `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}<category>`. Category literals: `"hallmarks"`, `"reliquaries"`, `"enshrines"`. Ark basenames: `image`, `about`, `vouch`, `pouch`, `diags`, `attest`. Attest is a **single package with per-platform tags**: `.../attest:<hallmark>-<ARCH>`.

## Binding vocabulary conversions

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-image` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/image:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-about` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-vouch` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/vouch:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-pouch` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/pouch:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-diags` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/diags:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-attest-<ARCH>` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/attest:<hallmark>-<ARCH>` |
| `{repo}/enshrine:{anchor}` | `<prefix>enshrines/<anchor>:<anchor>` |
| `vouches:<hallmark>-vouch` (aggregator) | DISSOLVED — hallmark-only lookup via subtree navigation |
| "vouches superdirectory/package/aggregator" phrase | rephrase as "hallmark subtree" or drop |
| `RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_*` constant | `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_*` |
| `_RBGA_ARK_SUFFIX_*` attribute ref | `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_*` |
| `RBGC_VOUCHES_PACKAGE` | DELETED (delete any attribute or reference) |

When a spec attribute refers to a deleted/renamed constant, delete/rename the row. When prose describes "hyphen-suffix tag", "tagged as `-image`", etc., rewrite to match basename/subtree shape.

## Enshrine shift (crucial for RBSAE)

Old: `{repo}/enshrine:{anchor}` — singular `enshrine` namespace, anchor was a Docker tag on a shared package. Multiple anchors shared one package's tag space.

New: `<prefix>enshrines/<anchor>:<anchor>` — plural `enshrines/` for namespace symmetry with `hallmarks/` and `reliquaries/`; anchor moves from tag to **path segment**. Each anchor is now a peer package under the namespace. The final `:<anchor>` tag matches the path segment (redundant-by-construction, divergence = visible bug).

## Your files (4 total)

1. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAB-ark_about.adoc`** — spec for the about artifact. ~20 hits of `-image/-about/-diags` references, plus an attribute table (lines ~128–135) with `_RBGA_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE/ABOUT/DIAGS` pointing at `RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_*` — these attributes must rename to `_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_*` and the "Source: `RBGC_*`" back-reference must rename to `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_*`. The prose discussing `-diags` as a conjure-only artifact remains conceptually intact; just update the tag grammar. Syft registry examples at ~lines 57–61 also need updating.

2. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc`** — spec for conjure. 5 hits: references to `-image` and `-about` artifacts produced by the build, `VESSEL:HALLMARK-pouch` push grammar, and `enshrine:«ANCHOR»` HEAD check. Update all to the new shape.

3. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc`** — spec for enshrine. Only 2 direct hits but the conceptual shift is substantial: the description of the `enshrine` namespace and `{repo}/enshrine:{anchor}` path must become `<prefix>enshrines/<anchor>:<anchor>`. Update prose to explain each anchor is now a peer package, not a tag on a shared package.

4. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAS-ark_summon.adoc`** — spec for summon. 3 hits (~lines 45, 47, 49): `VESSEL:HALLMARK-{image,about,vouch}` tags → basename shape.

## How to work

1. Read each file first. Understand the existing prose structure and tone.
2. Edit in place using the Edit tool. Preserve the asciidoc style: attribute refs, anchors, includes, `//axho*` section markers, bullet structure.
3. Don't add comments explaining changes. Don't add emojis.
4. For attribute table rows: rename both the attribute name AND the "Source:" back-reference in the same edit.
5. For RBSAE, the enshrine section may need prose restructuring to explain the anchor-as-path-segment shift, not just token replacement.

## When complete

3. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc`** — security posture spec. ## Binding path shapes (post-migration reality, do not deviate)

```
Arks:        ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/<hallmark>/<basename>:<hallmark>
Reliquaries: ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/<date>/<tool>:<date>
Enshrines:   ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/<anchor>:<anchor>
```

Root constants (RBGL_*) kindle from `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}<category>`. Category literals: `"hallmarks"`, `"reliquaries"`, `"enshrines"`. Ark basenames: `image`, `about`, `vouch`, `pouch`, `diags`, `attest`. Attest is a **single package with per-platform tags**: `.../attest:<hallmark>-<ARCH>`.

## Binding vocabulary conversions

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-image` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/image:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-about` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>` |

## Abjure rewrite specifics (RBSAA)

The actual implementation (`Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:264–359`) does this:

1. Authenticate as Director (same as before)
2. **NEW**: Use GAR REST API `GET /packages?pageSize=1000` to list all packages in the repository (not Docker Registry V2 manifests API)
3. Filter package names that start with `hallmarks/<hallmark>/` (URL-encoded with `%2F`)
4. If zero matches → "hallmark not present in registry" fatal
5. Confirm with user (unless `--force`) — show the count and list of packages to be deleted
6. For each filtered package → GAR REST API `DELETE /packages/<URL-encoded-name>`
7. Report count abjured

- Unit of delete: a **package**, not a manifest tag (deleting a package removes all its tags)

- The original "Construct Ark Tags" step (lines 72–88) collapses since tags aren't pre-constructed; enumeration discovers them

== Limitations

* Host platform only -- a kludge-built `linux/arm64` image on Apple Silicon will not run on `linux/amd64` hosts

toolUseResult.originalFile:
//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_ark_kludge}

NOTE: This operation builds a vessel image locally on the workstation using `docker build`, bypassing Cloud Build and GAR entirely. It produces a
{rbtga_hallmark}
with a `k` prefix (kludge) that composes cleanly with the nameplate and compose infrastructure but is obviously not a production hallmark.

NOTE: Kludge is a deliberate development shortcut for fast edit-build-test cycles. It enables iterating on vessel Dockerfiles, entrypoint scripts, and package lists without waiting for a Cloud Build round-trip. The ugly name is intentional -- this is not part of the production hallmark pipeline.

NOTE: The kludge hallmark format is `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»`, paralleling conjure's `c«YYMMDDHHMMSS»` and bind's `b«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`. The timestamp provides chronological sorting; the `git describe --always` suffix ties the image to a specific commit. The dirty-tree guard ensures the describe always corresponds to a committed state. The `k` prefix makes kludge hallmarks visually distinct in nameplate files and `docker image ls` output.

The kludge operation:

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{rbtga_ark}
. *Validate Input Parameters*:
.. {rbbc_require}
vessel provided as first argument (sigil or path)
.. {rbbc_require}
vessel is conjure mode (bind and graft vessels have no local Dockerfile)
.. {rbbc_require}
vessel has conjuring configuration (RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE, RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)

//axhos_step
. *Resolve Base Images*:
.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where
{rbrv_image_origin}
is set, use the origin directly as the `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-arg
.. No enshrine anchor resolution -- kludge pulls base images from upstream registries

//axhos_step
. *Assign Kludge Hallmark*:
.. Mint hallmark as `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»` from dispatch timestamp and `BURD_GIT_CONTEXT`
.. {rbbc_store}
`«HALLMARK»`

//axhos_step
. *Build Image Locally*:
.. Run `docker build` with resolved `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-args
.. Build for host platform only (no multi-arch -- development builds run where they are built)
.. Tag as `«GAR_BASE»/hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` to match compose YAML interpolation format

//axhos_step
. *Create Fake Vouch Tag*:
.. `docker tag` the image ref to also carry the `vouch` basename under the same hallmark subtree (`«GAR_BASE»/hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/vouch:«HALLMARK»`)
.. This is the same image aliased -- no provenance verification, no DSSE envelope
.. Satisfies the `rbob_charge` vouch gate (`docker image inspect` on vouch ref)

//axhos_step
. *Report Hallmark*:
.. Print the hallmark value for the operator to paste into the nameplate `.env` file
.. Persist hallmark to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` for downstream chaining

== Limitations

* Requires GCP depot configuration (foundry kindle validates `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE` exists, though kludge does not use credentials)
* Host platform only -- a kludge-built `linux/arm64` image on Apple Silicon will not run on `linux/amd64` hosts
* No about basename, no provenance chain -- the vouch basename is a same-image alias with no attestation content
* Not suitable for release or delivery -- use {rbtgo_ark_conjure} for production hallmarks

== Tabtarget

`tt/rbw-fk.LocalKludge.sh «vessel-sigil»`

== See Also

* {rbtgo_ark_conjure} -- production image build via Cloud Build
* {rbtgo_ark_vouch} -- provenance attestation (kludge bypasses this entirely)
* {rbtgo_ark_summon} -- pull from GAR (not needed for kludge -- image is already local)

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_abjure}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation formally renounces an
5	{rbtga_ark}
6	from
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	by enumerating and deleting every package beneath the ark's
9	hallmark subtree via the GAR REST API implemented by
10	{gar_service}.
11	
12	An
13	{rbtga_ark}
14	is the coherent unit of a Recipe Bottle build: a set of packages rooted at
15	`hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/` and distinguished by basename.
16	The
17	{rbtga_ark_image}
18	(basename `image`) contains the container image itself.
19	The
20	{rbtga_ark_about}
21	(basename `about`) contains SBOM and build metadata.
22	The
23	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
24	(basename `vouch`) contains verification results.
25	The
26	{rbtga_ark_pouch}
27	(basename `pouch`) contains the build context (conjure/bind only, absent for graft).
28	A fifth basename, `diags`, is present for conjure arks only (build-time diagnostics forwarded to the about pipeline).
29	For conjure vessels, a single `attest` package holds per-platform tags (`attest:«HALLMARK»-«ARCH»`).
30	Abjuring an ark deletes every package discovered beneath the hallmark subtree as a single logical operation.
31	
32	The
33	{rbtr_director}
34	abjures an ark:
35	
36	//axhopt_typed_parameter
37	{rbtga_ark}
38	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
39	.. {rbbc_require}
40	`«INPUT_VESSEL»` provided as first argument (retained for AAK signature compatibility; not used to construct the subtree path, drops in AAL)
41	.. {rbbc_require}
42	`«INPUT_HALLMARK»` provided as second argument
43	.. {rbbc_fatal}
44	if hallmark missing: "Hallmark parameter required"
45	
46	//axhopt_typed_parameter
47	{rbtga_hallmark}
48	. *Validate Hallmark Format*:
49	.. {rbbc_require}
50	`«INPUT_HALLMARK»` matches pattern `[cbg]\d{12}-r\d{12}` (mode prefix + dual timestamp)
51	.. {rbbc_fatal}
52	if malformed: "Hallmark format invalid"
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Authenticate as
56	{rbtr_director}*:
57	.. Execute
58	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
59	.. {rbbc_store}
60	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Construct Hallmark Subtree*:
64	.. {rbbc_store}
65	`«SUBTREE»` as `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/«INPUT_HALLMARK»/` where `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»` derives from `RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT` (kindled from `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` + `hallmarks`)
66	.. {rbbc_store}
67	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base (`projects/«PROJECT»/locations/«REGION»/repositories/«REPOSITORY»`)
68	
69	//axhos_step
70	. *Enumerate Packages Under Subtree*:
71	.. {rbbc_call}
72	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
73	... method: `GET`
74	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
75	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
76	.. {rbbc_require}
77	HTTP 200 response
78	.. Extract `.packages[].name` from the response, strip the resource prefix, and URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`
79	.. {rbbc_store}
80	`«ARK_PACKAGES»` as the list of decoded package names whose prefix equals `«SUBTREE»`
81	.. {rbbc_fatal}
82	if `«ARK_PACKAGES»` is empty: "No packages found under «SUBTREE» — hallmark not present in registry"
83	
84	//axhos_step
85	. *Confirm Abjuration*:
86	.. Unless `--force` flag provided:
87	... {rbbc_show}
88	"Will abjure «COUNT» packages under «SUBTREE»:" followed by each entry in `«ARK_PACKAGES»`
89	... {rbbc_require}
90	user types `yes` to confirm
91	... {rbbc_fatal}
92	if not confirmed: "prompt not confirmed."
93	
94	//axhos_step
95	. *Delete Each Enumerated Package*:
96	.. For each `«PACKAGE»` in `«ARK_PACKAGES»`:
97	... {rbbc_store}
98	`«ENCODED»` as `«PACKAGE»` with `/` replaced by `%2F`
99	... {rbbc_call}
100	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/delete[GAR REST API: packages.delete] with:
101	.... method: `DELETE`
102	.... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages/«ENCODED»`
103	.... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
104	... {rbbc_require}
105	HTTP 200 response (deletion returns a long-running operation; the 200 is treated as accepted)
106	... {rbbc_fatal}
107	if delete fails: "Failed to delete package «PACKAGE» (HTTP «STATUS_CODE»)"
108	
109	//axhoot_typed_output
110	{rbtga_ark}
111	. *Display Results*:
112	.. {rbbc_show}
113	"Hallmark abjured: «INPUT_HALLMARK» («COUNT» packages)"
114	.. For each deleted package:
115	... {rbbc_show}
116	`  - «PACKAGE» deleted`
117	
118	//axhoc_completion
119	Procedure completes successfully when every package discovered beneath the hallmark subtree has been deleted from the registry and the abjuration confirmation is displayed.
120	The ark is now formally renounced and no longer part of the repository corpus.
121	Because enumeration drives the delete set, there is no separate existence check and no orphan-detection branch: whatever lives in the subtree is removed, and an empty subtree is itself the "not present" signal.
122	Deleting a package removes all of its tags in one call — per-basename HEAD/DELETE dances are no longer required.
123

content.text:
**Notch 3 mechanically complete.** All 7 binding grep-zero checks pass. Summary:

**Work delivered:**
- 17 spec files edited across 5 parallel agents (~40 min wall-clock including grep pass, reslate, dispatch, verification)
- RBSAA algorithm section collapsed from per-suffix HEAD/DELETE dance (241 lines) to enumerate-then-delete via GAR REST `/packages` (122 lines) — reads cleanly, accurately describes `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:264–358`
- RBSAV aggregator dissolution clean: `_RBGV_VOUCHES_PACKAGE` row deleted, dual-tag grammar dropped, subtree-navigation prose added
- RBSAE enshrine shift prose (anchor path segment + tag-matches-segment invariant)
- RBSCB tag table rebuilt to show full `<prefix>hallmarks/<H>/<basename>:<H>` shape
- RBSCL + RBSDV classification algorithms rewritten to key off basename presence instead of tag-suffix matching
- RBSDI got `_RBGN_CLOUD_PREFIX` substitution docs + new reliquary path example

**Fixes I did myself after the agent pass:**
- RBS0 line 3147: Agent A missed an enshrine-namespace reference at the regime variable definition site — updated to `enshrines/«anchor»:«anchor»` shape
- RBSAK line 58: awkward "No about basename" phrasing → "No about artifact" (the structural term didn't fit the absence statement)

**Flagged for potential follow-up (not blocking):**
- Agent B noted `{rbf_fact_ark_stem}` definition was reshaped in specs — if the code-side fact file stores something different, spec/code could drift. Worth a spot check but outside Notch 3 scope.
- Agent A used shape-level verb "probe" at RBS0 ~line 1832 re: tally detection mechanism; RBSCL (rewritten by Agent D) is the authoritative description, so cross-reference is consistent.

**Not run:** fast/crucible test suites. Spec-only changes; no bash/Rust code modified. The test-gate contract from the docket ("fast suite mandatory pre-notch") is procedurally satisfied since Notch 2b already banked a 90/90 green run, but if you want belt-and-braces confirmation before notching, I can kick off the fast suite now.

Ready to notch Notch 3 of ₢A_AAK when you confirm. Do you want the fast suite run first, or proceed directly to `jjx_record`?

params.intent: Notch 3 of ₢A_AAK — spec sweep completing the GAR categorical-layout migration. 18 spec files updated to match the Notch 2b code cutover. Heaviest change is RBSAA: the abjure algorithm description collapses from a per-suffix HEAD/DELETE dance (241 lines, 5-6 hardcoded suffixes, per-artifact existence + orphan-detection branches + individual DELETE steps) to enumerate-then-delete via GAR REST /packages (122 lines, single list-filter-loop-delete flow that naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch). RBSAV dissolves the vouches aggregator: _RBGV_VOUCHES_PACKAGE attribute row deleted entirely, dual-tag push grammar (VESSEL:HALLMARK-vouch + vouches:HALLMARK-vouch) replaced by single package under hallmarks/HALLMARK/vouch with subtree navigation for hallmark-only lookup. RBSAE reflects the enshrine anchor-as-path-segment shift — the `enshrine:anchor` tag-on-shared-package grammar becomes `enshrines/anchor:anchor` peer packages under a plural namespace, symmetric with hallmarks/ and reliquaries/. RBSCB rebuilds its Cloud Build tag-scheme table to show full <prefix>hallmarks/HALLMARK/<basename>:HALLMARK shape with a new attest row capturing the single-package-with-per-platform-tags pattern. RBSCL and RBSDV classification algorithms key off basename presence under hallmark subtrees instead of tag-suffix matching; health-state semantics (vouched/pending/incomplete) preserved. RBSDI gains `_RBGN_CLOUD_PREFIX` Cloud Build substitution docs plus the new reliquaries/<date>/<tool>:<date> path example. The remaining 12 files receive proportionally lighter edits: basename-shape tag grammar in RBSAC/RBSAS/RBSAG/RBSAK/RBSIJ/RBSCC, subtree-navigation prose in RBSAP/RBSGS replacing the vouches-superdirectory mechanism, attribute renames in RBSAB (_RBGA_ARK_SUFFIX_* → _RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_*) and RBSTB (_RBGY_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE → _RBGY_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE) with their RBGC_* back-references. Two fixes applied after the agent pass: RBS0:3147 where the regime variable definition site still referenced `enshrine:«anchor»` (missed by Agent A who focused on the attestation section), and RBSAK:58 where agent output read 'No about basename, no provenance chain' — rephrased to 'No about artifact' since structural-location terminology ('basename') doesn't fit an absence statement. All 7 binding verification greps return zero: RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_, RBGC_VOUCHES_PACKAGE, :${HALLMARK}-(image|vouch|pouch|about|diags) hyphen-suffix concat, enshrine:anchor old tag grammar, 'vouches (superdirectory|package|aggregator)' aggregator phrasing, «HALLMARK»-(image|vouch|pouch|about|diags) stale substitution grammar, and {vessel}:{hallmark}-X. Two flagged follow-ups captured but not blocking: Agent B reshaped the {rbf_fact_ark_stem} fact description to match the new grammar; if the code-side rbf_fact value actually stores something different, spec/code could drift (worth a future spot check). Agent A used verb 'probe' in RBS0 ~1832 re: tally detection mechanism without inspecting rbfl_tally implementation; RBSCL (Agent D) is the authoritative description and reads consistently. Spec-only change — no bash/Rust modified, no test suite run (Notch 2b already banked 90/90 fast-suite green). Closes Notch 3 of the 3-notch commit sequence in the groomed AAK plan; AAK's spec-side contract is now complete.

**Blocked — size guard:** 77,555 bytes staged, 50,000 limit.

The 18-file sweep is homogeneous work (all spec prose, one logical Notch 3 from the groomed 3-notch plan), which argues for one commit. But that's 55% over the limit.

**Options:**

1. **Raise limit to ~80000** — pass `size_limit` to keep Notch 3 as a single commit matching the groomed plan. One-line change to my record call.

2. **Split into 2 notches** — natural cut is by file size:
   - **Notch 3a** (~48K, 4 files): RBS0 (21.5K), RBSAA (12K), RBSAB (8.7K), RBSCB (5.8K) — the heaviest restructures (algorithm rewrite, tag table, attestation-section prose)
   - **Notch 3b** (~30K, 14 files): all lighter mechanical rewrites (RBSAV, RBSAP, RBSDV, RBSCL, RBSAC, RBSAE, RBSAG, RBSAK, RBSAS, RBSCC, RBSDI, RBSGS, RBSIJ, RBSTB)

3. **Split into 3 notches** — if you want every commit well under the default limit.

My preference is **option 1** (raise the limit) — the work is one coherent sweep, cross-file consistency was the whole reason we batched the agent dispatch, and splitting now would fragment that coherence across commits for archival reasons only. But the guard exists for a reason, so your call.

Which do you want?

The remaining 12 files receive proportionally lighter edits: basename-shape tag grammar in RBSAC/RBSAS/RBSAG/RBSAK/RBSIJ/RBSCC, subtree-navigation prose in RBSAP/RBSGS replacing the vouches-superdirectory mechanism, attribute renames in RBSAB (_RBGA_ARK_SUFFIX_* → _RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_*) and RBSTB (_RBGY_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE → _RBGY_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE) with their RBGC_* back-references. content.text:
Notch 3 committed as `93c651f2` (warnings only, non-blocking).

Before wrapping, let me address the one concrete follow-up Agent B flagged — `{rbf_fact_ark_stem}`. Quick check.

**Material finding — not wrap-ready.**

The `{rbf_fact_ark_stem}` check uncovered a scope miss from Notch 2b: Rust-side crucible code still has old hyphen-suffix grammar.

**`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`:**
```
Line 2162: const RBTDRC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE: &str = "-image";
Line 2163: const RBTDRC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH: &str = "-vouch";
```

Used at 4 sites producing invalid locators post-migration:

| Line | Current output shape | Required shape |
|---|---|---|
| 2264 | `<hallmarks_root>/<hallmark>-image` | `<hallmarks_root>/<hallmark>/image:<hallmark>` |
| 2304 | `<gar_root>/<vessel>:<consec>-image` | `<gar_root>/<hallmarks_root>/<consec>/image:<consec>` |
| 2305 | `<gar_root>/<vessel>:<consec>-vouch` | `<gar_root>/<hallmarks_root>/<consec>/vouch:<consec>` |
| 2358 | `<vessel>:<consec>-image` | `<hallmarks_root>/<consec>/image:<consec>` |

**Why Notch 2b missed it:** The Notch 2b verification greps targeted `RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_` and bash-string-concat patterns (`:${HALLMARK}-`). These are Rust constants with their own prefix (`RBTDRC_ARK_SUFFIX_`), invisible to those greps. The Notch 2b commit message explicitly noted "service and crucible suites unblocked but not run from this officium (no live credentials)" — so the failure didn't surface at test time either.

**Impact:** The crucible suite (117 cases) will fail these locators when it runs against migrated GAR. The fast suite (which was 90/90 green) doesn't exercise these code paths — it's regime/enrollment validation, not crucible wiring.

**The AAK docket's core promise** — "Hard cutover — old and new path shapes do not coexist in live code" — is violated until this is fixed. AAK isn't complete.

**Options:**

1. **Add Notch 4 to AAK** — rewrite rbtdrc_crucible.rs:2159-2358 to use the new grammar, run the crucible suite via foray (or flag for user-side run). Keeps AAK's atomic-cutover contract honest.

2. **Spinoff pace under AAK** — slate a new pace like `rbtdrc-layout-catchup` dedicated to this. Keeps AAK's 3-notch plan clean in the log but leaves AAK formally incomplete in spirit.

3. **Wrap AAK as-is with explicit flag** — close with a "known gap: rbtdrc_crucible.rs" note and open a follow-up pace. Cleanest commit history; weakest on promise-keeping.

My preference is **option 1** — the work is small (one Rust file, 4 sites, one pair of constants to rename/remove), and the cutover contract is AAK's whole point. Option 2 is defensible if you want to keep Notch 3 as the "specs" notch and track Rust-side work as structurally distinct. Option 3 I'd avoid — the whole docket frames atomic cutover as the forcing function.

Which do you want?

content.content:
50	
51	######################################################################
52	# Public Functions (rbfr_*)
53	
54	rbfr_wrest() {
55	  zrbfr_sentinel
56	
57	  local z_locator="${1:-}"
58	
59	  # Documentation block
60	  buc_doc_brief "Wrest an image from the registry to local container runtime by locator"
61	  buc_doc_param "locator" "Image locator in package-path:tag format (e.g. hallmarks/H/image:H)"
62	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
63	
64	  # Validate locator parameter
65	  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Locator parameter required (package-path:tag)"
66	
67	  # Parse locator into package path and tag
68	  case "${z_locator}" in
69	    *:*) : ;;
70	    *)   buc_die "Invalid locator format. Expected package-path:tag" ;;
71	  esac
72	  local z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"
73	  local z_tag="${z_locator##*:}"
74	  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in locator"
75	  test -n "${z_tag}" || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"
76	
77	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
78	
79	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
80	
81	  # Get OAuth token
82	  local z_token
83	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
84	
85	  buc_step "Logging into container registry"
86	
87	  # Construct full image reference
88	  local z_full_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${z_locator}"
89	
90	  # Docker login to GAR
91	  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}" \
92	    || buc_die "Container runtime authentication failed"
93	
94	  buc_step "Pulling image: ${z_full_ref}"
95	
96	  # Pull image
97	  docker pull "${z_full_ref}" || buc_die "Image pull failed"
98	
99	  # Get local image ID
100	  local z_image_id
101	  docker inspect --format='{{.Id}}' "${z_full_ref}" > "${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null \
102	    || buc_die "Failed to get image ID"
103	  z_image_id=$(<"${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}")
104	
105	  # Display results
106	  echo ""
107	  echo "Image wrested: ${z_full_ref}"
108	  echo "Local image ID: ${z_image_id}"
109	
110	  buc_success "Image wrest complete"
111	}
112	
113	rbfr_summon() {
114	  zrbfr_sentinel
115	
116	  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
117	  z_vessel="${z_vessel##*/}"  # strip path prefix — accept directory path or bare moniker
118	  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"

######################################################################
# Public Functions (rbfr_*)

rbfr_wrest() {
  zrbfr_sentinel

  local z_locator="${1:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Wrest an image from the registry to local container runtime by locator"
  buc_doc_param "locator" "Image locator in package-path:tag format (e.g. hallmarks/H/image:H)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Validate locator parameter
  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Locator parameter required (package-path:tag)"

  # Parse locator into package path and tag
  case "${z_locator}" in
    *:*) : ;;
    *)   buc_die "Invalid locator format. Expected package-path:tag" ;;
  esac
  local z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"
  local z_tag="${z_locator##*:}"
  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in locator"
  test -n "${z_tag}" || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"

  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"

  # Get OAuth token
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  buc_step "Logging into container registry"

  # Construct full image reference
  local z_full_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${z_locator}"

  # Docker login to GAR
  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}" \
    || buc_die "Container runtime authentication failed"

  buc_step "Pulling image: ${z_full_ref}"

  # Pull image
  docker pull "${z_full_ref}" || buc_die "Image pull failed"

  # Get local image ID
  local z_image_id
  docker inspect --format='{{.Id}}' "${z_full_ref}" > "${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null \
    || buc_die "Failed to get image ID"
  z_image_id=$(<"${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}")

  # Display results
  echo ""
  echo "Image wrested: ${z_full_ref}"
  echo "Local image ID: ${z_image_id}"

  buc_success "Image wrest complete"
}

rbfr_summon() {
  zrbfr_sentinel

  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
  z_vessel="${z_vessel##*/}"  # strip path prefix — accept directory path or bare moniker
  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Summon an ark (pull -image, -about, and -vouch artifacts as a coherent unit)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel name (e.g., rbev-busybox)"
  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_log_args "Validate parameters"
  rbfc_require_vessel_sigil "${z_vessel}"

  buc_step "Authenticating for retrieval"
  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  # Hallmark required — use rbw-ft (tally) to discover available hallmarks
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark parameter required (use rbw-ft to tally vouched hallmarks)"

  # Ark package paths — all basename siblings under the hallmark subtree
  local -r z_image_pkg="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}"
  local -r z_about_pkg="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}"
  local -r z_vouch_pkg="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}"

  buc_step "Verifying ark existence"

  # Check if image ark exists (tag = hallmark)
  local z_image_status_file="${ZRBFR_TEMP_PREFIX}summon_image_status.txt"
  local z_image_response_file="${ZRBFR_TEMP_PREFIX}summon_image_response.json"

  curl --head -s                                     \
    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"           \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"           \
    -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}"     \
    -w "%{http_code}"                               \
    -o "${z_image_response_file}"                   \
    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_image_pkg}/manifests/${z_hallmark}" \
    > "${z_image_status_file}" || buc_die "HEAD request failed for image ark"

  local z_image_http_code
  z_image_http_code=$(<"${z_image_status_file}")
  test -n "${z_image_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for image ark"

  local z_image_exists=false
  if test "${z_image_http_code}" = "200"; then
    z_image_exists=true

(3) 7 CLI scripts (rbfc_cli, rbfd_cli, rbfl_cli, rbfr_cli, rbfv_cli, rbob_cli, rbrn_cli) source rbgl_GarLayout.sh immediately after rbgc_Constants.sh and invoke zrbgl_kindle immediately after zrbgc_kindle (ordering matters — rbgl kindle takes zrbgc_sentinel dependency for category constants). rbrn_cli places source+kindle inside the heavy survey/audit conditional branch. (4) Consumer module rewrites for sites that don't involve Cloud Build substitution plumbing: rbob_bottle.sh ZRBOB_SENTRY/BOTTLE_IMAGE/VOUCH construction uses $\{RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT\}/$\{HALLMARK\}/$\{RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_X\}:$\{HALLMARK\} pattern (hallmark-as-tag); rbrn_cli.sh z_sentry_img/z_bottle_img same pattern; rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh summon rewrote to: (a) ark package paths as z_image_pkg/z_about_pkg/z_vouch_pkg locals built inline from constants, (b) HEAD URLs use package path + manifests/$\{hallmark\}, (c) pull refs use full URL with tag=hallmark, (d) locator parsing in rbfr_wrest uses last-colon split (%:* and ##*:) for path-may-contain-colons shapes, (e) simplified list-vouched-hallmarks branch replaced by die-with-pointer-to-rbw-ft (the hallmark-discovery feature needed _catalog-based rewrite — deferred as scope, tally command handles the use case). rbfc_FoundryCore.sh: zrbfc_resolve_tool_images assigns tool refs from RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT + RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_* constants with tag=$\{reliquary_date\} (immutable-datestamp-as-tag, matches hallmark-as-tag discipline); plumb hallmark-only resolution branch dissolves vouches-superdirectory fetch in favor of fetching hallmark's own vouch ark (vouch_summary.json still contains vessel field — verified no producer change needed); plumb about/vouch fetches use $\{RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT\}/$\{hallmark\}/$\{basename\} package paths with tag=hallmark. (5) BCG-compliance cleanup collateral: ZRBFC_TOOL_{GCLOUD,DOCKER,ALPINE,SYFT,BINFMT,SKOPEO} renamed to z_rbfc_tool_* across rbfc (13 sites) and rbfd (10 sites). These variables are assigned in zrbfc_resolve_tool_images (not kindle — they depend on RBRV_RELIQUARY which comes from vessel-load), so per BCG 'Mutable kindle state' rule they must be lowercase z_-prefixed (SCREAMING_SNAKE is reserved for readonly kindle constants). Initialized empty in zrbfc_kindle under a 'Tool image refs — mutable kindle state' comment. This was a LATENT pre-existing BCG violation that became my scope because I was editing those specific lines for AAK. NOT TOUCHED IN THIS NOTCH (deferred to Notch 2b): rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh 15+ sites (image_base construction line 596, per-platform attest URI loop line 602, conjure jq substitution block lines 622-692, pouch context tag line 733, bind image/vouch refs lines 1260-1261, bind image tag 1371, per-platform attest fact writes 1180-1182, mirror/bind jq substitution block 1474-1522, graft image tag 1632, graft fact write 1665, enshrine jq block 856-884 including _RBGE_ENSHRINE_NAME that currently carries $\{CLOUD_PREFIX\}enshrine and needs reshape to plural enshrines/ with anchor-as-path-segment not tag); rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh 22+ sites (tag construction lines 75/130/154/212/371/374/395-396/605/629, 3 verify jq substitution blocks for graft/bind/conjure at lines 317-358/510-533/729-754 — each block currently passes _RBGV_VOUCHES_PACKAGE which dissolves, catalog-filter case statement lines 852/855 that switches on SUFFIX patterns); rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh abjure per-suffix HEAD/DELETE dance at lines 282-341 collapses to enumerate-then-delete via gcloud artifacts packages list against hallmark subtree (naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch — no special case); .rbk/rbob_compose.yml 3 image references at lines 23/57/77 reshape to $\{CLOUD_PREFIX\}hallmarks/$\{HALLMARK\}/image:$\{HALLMARK\}; Cloud Build step scripts (rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh line 36 + dispatcher — needs _RBGN_CLOUD_PREFIX substitution threading and reliquary tag change from :latest to :$\{RELIQUARY_DATE\}; rbgje/rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh line 62 — _RBGE_ENSHRINE_NAME reshape to plural + anchor-as-tag; 6 rbgjb/ scripts and 2 rbgja/ and 2 rbgjv/ scripts — drop _RBG?_ARK_SUFFIX_* substitutions, add _RBG?_HALLMARKS_ROOT substitution, consumer scripts build URL as $\{_RBG?_HALLMARKS_ROOT\}/$\{_RBG?_HALLMARK\}/<basename-literal>:$\{_RBG?_HALLMARK\}). Grep-zero discipline currently UNSATISFIED: grep -nE 'RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_|RBGC_VOUCHES_PACKAGE' hits ~50 sites across rbfd/rbfv/rbfl pointing to now-undefined readonly constants; the foundry modules will die at function-body execution time if their path-construction code is reached. Fast suite doesn't exercise those paths so tree is green on fast (47+21+7+15 = 90/90). Service and crucible suites will fail until Notch 2b lands. rbhodf tutorial was swept into ₣A6 commit 5c9522d5 via multi-officium concurrency overlap (another officium's jjx_record explicit file list included rbhodf and my WIP edits rode along) — the BASENAME/CATEGORY_HALLMARKS rewrites are live in the tree but attributed to the other heat; no action needed in this notch or in 2b. Grooming decisions preserved in pace docket: rbgl is constants-only (deviated from 5-helper-functions plan — docket Decisions section 1-2 now effectively describes constants instead of functions); hallmark-as-tag with 'tag = enclosing directory's immutable identifier' pattern extended to reliquary datestamp and enshrine anchor; abjure converts to enumerate-then-delete (gcloud API level, not subtree delete — GAR doesn't offer that); Notch 3 handles specs (RBSAC/RBSAE/RBSDI/RBSAA/RBSAV/RBSAS/RBSAP/RBSCL/RBS0/RBSCO). BCG lessons discovered mid-flight that must be forward-applied to Notch 2b: (a) no $() invocation on non-_capture functions — use pure-interpolation kindle constants or guarded _capture; (b) yawp is for human-readable text fragments only, NOT internal computation; (c) SCREAMING_SNAKE is for readonly kindle constants — mutable kindle state assigned outside kindle must be z_-lowercase; (d) named RBGC_* constants for system-vocabulary literals (image/vouch/about/pouch/attest/diags/gcloud/docker/alpine/syft/binfmt/skopeo) — magic strings hide meaning; (e) for Cloud Build consumer scripts that can't source rbgl, pass HALLMARKS_ROOT as a Cloud Build substitution (not function-call result) and let consumer do pure interpolation. Verification landed: bash -n clean on all 13 files; fast suite 90/90 green; shellcheck -S error clean on rbgl + rbgc.
d96f3086 jjb:1011-78baf888:₢A_AAB:n: Thread RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX into local container/network names and RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX into GAR path construction at vessel position across the foundry family. Runtime side: rbob_bottle.sh kindle constants (ZRBOB_SENTRY/PENTACLE/BOTTLE/NETWORK) and compose -p project arg carry the prefix so compose's generated {project}_enclave network name matches ZRBOB_NETWORK; compose.yml container_name directives interpolate RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX. Cloud side: RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX inserted at GAR-internal vessel-path position across bash sites (rbob GAR image/vouch refs, rbrn survey image refs, rbfr wrest locator + 4 API URLs + 3 image refs, rbfv 9 manifests URLs + z_vi_gar_prefix x2, rbfc zrbfc_resolve_tool_images reliquary prefix + zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact callers, rbfd reliquary canary + enshrine HEAD + enshrine image prefix + sigil path + context tag + kludge/graft image refs, rbfl 5 HEAD + 5 DELETE + 2 tags/list + jettison manifest) and baked into jq-arg substitutions feeding Cloud Build step scripts (_RBGA_VESSEL via zjq_vessel/zjq_moniker, _RBGV_VESSEL, _RBGY_MONIKER, _RBGN_RELIQUARY, _RBGV_VOUCHES_PACKAGE). Compose.yml image refs prepend RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX at vessel segment. Enshrine category: new _RBGE_ENSHRINE_NAME substitution in rbfd's enshrine jq block carrying ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}enshrine; rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh consumes it to replace the bare 'enshrine' literal (parallel to how _RBGN_RELIQUARY carries the prefixed reliquary datestamp). All path threading is AAK-disposable — AAK reshapes path grammar entirely into <prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/... categorical layout, so AAB keeps the current vessel:hallmark-suffix shape and inserts prefix at vessel position only, zero structural change. Fixed adjacent pre-existing typo in rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:653: RBGC_vouches_PACKAGE -> RBGC_VOUCHES_PACKAGE (misspelled variable name in hallmark-only plumb lookup — silently fell through to 'Hallmark not found in vouches superdirectory' die branch under set -u, surfaced while threading the prefix through that line). Verification: fast suite 90/90 green (47 enrollment-validation + 21 regime-validation + 7 regime-smoke + 15 handbook-render); nameplate render and nameplate-info survey exercise RBRN image-ref computation cleanly with empty prefix; RBRR repo regime render surfaces both prefix vars with '(not set)' indicator. Crucible suite not run here (requires container runtime) — baseline-preservation property of empty-default makes regression risk low, but worth running against a live fundus before final sign-off.

file.content:
# Generate kludge hallmark (k prefix distinguishes from conjure c, bind b)
  # Timestamp for chronological sorting, git describe for commit provenance
  # BURD_GIT_CONTEXT is exported by bud_dispatch; dirty-tree guard above ensures clean tree
  local -r z_hallmark="k${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}-${BURD_GIT_CONTEXT}"

  # Construct image refs matching compose/vouch-gate format (new layout — hallmark-as-tag).
  local -r z_image_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"
  local -r z_vouch_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"

  buc_step "Kludge build: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
  buc_info "Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_info "Image tag: ${z_image_ref}"

  # Build locally (host platform only — no multi-arch for dev builds)
  buc_step "Building image locally"
  docker build \
    "${z_build_args[@]}" \
    -f "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}" \
    -t "${z_image_ref}" \
    "${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}" \
    || buc_die "Local build failed for ${RBRV_SIGIL}"

  # Create fake vouch tag (same image, aliased — satisfies rbob_charge vouch gate)
  buc_step "Creating vouch tag"
  docker tag "${z_image_ref}" "${z_vouch_ref}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create vouch tag"

  # Persist hallmark to output directory
  buf_write_fact "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge build complete: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
  buc_bare ""
  buc_bare "  Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_bare "  Image:        ${z_image_ref}"
  buc_bare "  Vouch:        ${z_vouch_ref}"
  buc_bare ""
  buc_bare "  Auto-install via: tt/rbw-tK.KludgeCycle.{moniker}.sh"
}

params.intent: Notch 4 of ₢A_AAK — Rust-side catchup for the GAR categorical-layout migration. Discovered during Notch 3 spec review: rbtdrc_crucible.rs (the four-mode foundry integration test fixture) still carried the old hyphen-suffix grammar via its own Rust-side RBTDRC_ARK_SUFFIX_{IMAGE,VOUCH} constants, invisible to Notch 2b's grep-zero verification which targeted the bash-side RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_ prefix. Four call sites were emitting invalid post-migration locators: wrest-for-graft input, kludge image/vouch local docker-store refs, and final conjured-image wrest+run. This would surface as crucible-suite failures since the fixture's locators no longer matched what rbfd_kludge and compose.yml actually produce; fast suite didn't catch it because rbtdrc_crucible.rs is only exercised by the crucible suite (117 cases, requires container runtime). Root cause for why Notch 2b missed it: the Rust binary defined its own constants mirroring the bash values; the 2b sweep greped for the bash-prefix symbols (RBGC_/RBGL_) and for bash-level ':${HALLMARK}-' concat patterns, neither of which touches Rust code. Fix scope: three code files plus two spec alignment fixes. (1) rbtdrc_crucible.rs: removed RBTDRC_ARK_SUFFIX_{IMAGE,VOUCH}; added RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_{IMAGE,VOUCH} and RBTDRC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS matching their RBGC_ counterparts; rewrote the four call sites to emit the new locator grammar. Wrest locators (relative-to-cloud-prefix since rbfr_wrest prepends RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX itself): 'hallmarks/<hallmark>/<basename>:<hallmark>'. Full local refs (prefixed, match what wrest pulled to local cache): '<gar_root>/<prefixed_ark_stem>/<basename>:<hallmark>'. Per-mode symmetry enforced: each mode now reads its own HALLMARK + GAR_ROOT + ARK_STEM facts and builds refs uniformly. (2) rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh: added GAR_ROOT and ARK_STEM fact emissions to rbfd_kludge to match conjure/bind/graft's fact contract — previously kludge only emitted HALLMARK, forcing Rust consumers to reconstruct the stem via string manipulation on conjure's fact. Per-mode fact emission is now uniform; downstream consumers read the same three facts regardless of mode. (3) RBS0-SpecTop.adoc:1884: fixed stale 'Ark stem (vessel:hallmark)' description that Agent A missed during Notch 3 — rewrote to match the actual emission shape '«PREFIX»hallmarks/«HALLMARK»'. (4) RBSAG-ark_graft.adoc:114: aligned ark_stem description with RBSAC's wording — Agent C described the stem without cloud prefix, Agent B described it with; the bash truth (includes prefix via RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT) matches Agent B. Verification: cargo build succeeded (theurge crate, dev profile); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh returns 90/90 green (47 enrollment-validation + 21 regime-validation + 7 regime-smoke + 15 handbook-render) matching Notch 2b baseline with no regression. Crucible suite (which exercises the edited fixture) not run — requires container runtime and live GAR credentials; flagging for user-side or foray-based verification in the close summary of AAK. The hard-cutover contract is now genuinely complete: no old grammar survives in live code paths. Two flagged follow-ups from Notch 3 remain: (a) Agent A's 'probe' verb choice in RBS0 ~1832 (shape-level; RBSCL is the authoritative description so cross-reference reads consistent); (b) the wrest locator contract split — fact_ark_stem includes cloud prefix, wrest's locator input does not — surfaces as a Rust-side conceptual tax (Rust builds two different string forms from the same semantic concept). A future cleanup could unify (e.g., split into RELATIVE_STEM + PREFIX facts or fold cloud prefix into GAR_ROOT). Scope-out for AAK: the contract asymmetry doesn't produce incorrect behavior, just more format! calls. Closes ₢A_AAK's cutover-discipline contract.

content.text:
Notch 4 committed as `739b4a35`. ₢A_AAK's cutover-discipline contract is now genuinely complete.

**On the testing question: yes, slate a testing pace next — before `₢A_AAE`.**

Here's the reasoning.

**What we've actually validated across AAK:**
- Fast suite 90/90 green after Notch 2b and again after Notch 4
- Grep-zero on stale-grammar markers across Tools/ and .rbk/
- Rust crate compiles

**What we haven't validated (the iceberg):**

| Surface | Exercised? | Risk if broken |
|---|---|---|
| Cloud Build step scripts (rbgja/rbgjb/rbgjv/rbgje/rbgji — substitution-driven URL construction) | No | Build submissions fail with cryptic URL errors |
| Compose.yml interpolation (new `hallmarks/<H>/image:<H>` shape) | No | Charge breaks — sentry and bottle can't start |
| `rbfr_summon` real-GAR pull with new package layout | No | Retrieval silently pulls wrong thing or 404s |
| `rbfv_FoundryVerify` vouch production on new layout | No | Vouches fail to produce or produce invalid content |
| `rbfl_abjure` new enumerate-then-delete against real GAR `/packages` API | No | Abjure leaks packages or fails mid-delete |
| `rbfl_tally` + `rbfl_rekon` + `rbfl_jettison` real-GAR paths | No | Director-side inventory broken |
| End-to-end ordain cycle (conjure → GAR → summon → charge → attack) | No | Any of the above compounds |
| Kludge local cache integration (now verified by Rust but not human) | Indirectly | Dev path broken — will catch attention fast |

The fast suite is regime/enrollment validation — it never touches GAR, never runs docker, never submits Cloud Build jobs. It's a correctness gate on kindle/sentinel discipline, not an integration gate.

**AAK's docket itself acknowledges this gap:** the Verification section lists crucible suite 117/117 "via foray or user-side" as a close-time requirement. Notch 2b's commit explicitly noted "service and crucible suites unblocked but not run from this officium (no live credentials)." That outstanding obligation is still outstanding.

**On the depot regen pace (₢A_AAE):**

The paddock framed it as "the hard cutover point — all three threads land before it; depot regen validates the new infrastructure in regenerated form." That phrasing is easy to misread as "regen is the test." It's not — regen is the **irreversible** step that exercises the new code against a fresh depot. If the new code is broken, regen blows up after you've already deleted the old depot. No fallback.

So the sane order is:
1. **Slate a testing pace** — exercise the migration against the *existing* depot while it still stands as fallback. Run service suite, crucible suite (117 cases including the four-mode fixture I just fixed), a hand-run ordain of one vessel, and a hand-run summon+charge of tadmor. If anything's broken, you still have the working depot to diagnose against.
2. **Then ₢A_AAE** — depot regen, with confidence that the code paths actually work.
3. **Other paces** (₢A_AAL signature simplification, ₢A_AAJ moorings rename) can interleave or come after — they don't block the test/regen ordering.

**Proposed testing-pace silks:** `aak-integration-verify` or `aak-real-gar-burn-in`. Character: "live-infrastructure verification — the moment of truth for a migration that was all bash/Rust grammar until now."

**Ready to wrap ₢A_AAK?** If so, I can wrap, then we slate the testing pace. Or if you'd rather rail/reorder first to make the testing pace the next actionable (ahead of other remaining paces), say so and I'll do that before the wrap.

message.content: notch now.  Then slate the testing pace with current depot.  That should work, right?

content.text:
Notch 4 already committed (`739b4a35`) — nothing more of mine is uncommitted. I think you meant **wrap**, but the discipline says I must confirm before `jjx_close`.

**On "That should work, right?" — yes.** Testing against the current depot is the correct ordering. The existing depot has pre-migration data shapes coexisting with new code shapes in GAR (since packages persist until explicitly abjured), so the test should exercise:

- **Tally / plumb** against a depot containing *both* old-shape and new-shape hallmarks — catches any consumer path that can't handle mixed shapes
- **Fresh conjure + summon + charge + abjure** cycle — proves the new paths produce what consumers expect
- **Crucible suite (117 cases)** — exercises the four-mode fixture I just fixed plus tadmor-security adversarial tests
- **Abjure an old-shape hallmark** — proves enumerate-then-delete handles both shapes via subtree enumeration (it should — it's topology-agnostic — but worth confirming)

The existing depot is actually a *better* test surface than a regenerated one for exactly this reason: mixed-shape content exercises the consumer paths under more conditions. A fresh regen would only see new-shape content.

**One caveat worth flagging before you commit to this plan:** if old-shape hallmarks are expected to keep working long-term (i.e., no imminent abjure-all), the consumer code needs to handle old shapes gracefully. It currently doesn't — Notch 2b was atomic cutover. So some pre-AAK hallmarks in the current depot may be unreadable to post-AAK consumers. If that's the case, the test cycle should include abjuring all pre-AAK hallmarks before a clean-baseline run. Depends on what you have in the depot now.

# jjezs_slate aak-live-depot-burn-in

## Character

Live-infrastructure verification. The AAK migration was all grammar until now — bash string construction, Rust constants, spec prose, fast-suite regime checks. None of it has touched real GAR, real Cloud Build, real containers. This pace is the first-time burn-in: exercise every code path that matters, against the current depot (mixed-shape content is fine — no backward compat obligation), and surface any gaps before `₢A_AAE` (depot regen, irreversible).

Posture: investigative. Expect some surprises. Fix what's small inline; surface what's big for a follow-up pace. Don't force green — honest failures here are the whole point of running.

## Docket

Exercise the full AAK-migrated code surface against the current depot. Work through the tiers in order; escalate on failure.

### Tier 1 — Automated suites

- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.service.sh` — 80 cases (fast + access-probe + four-mode foundry integration). Requires GCP credentials at `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE` and `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`.
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.crucible.sh` — 117 cases (fast + tadmor-security + srjcl-jupyter + pluml-diagram). Requires container runtime.

Sequential only — fixtures share regime state and container namespaces.

### Tier 2 — Hand-run producer paths

Once suites are green (or triaged), exercise the director-side producer lifecycle against a real vessel:

- **Inscribe** a reliquary: `tt/rbw-dI.DirectorInscribesReliquary.sh` — verifies `_RBGN_CLOUD_PREFIX` substitution threads correctly into the inscribe builder; check resulting GAR shape under `<prefix>reliquaries/<date>/<tool>:<date>`.
- **Conjure** busybox: `tt/rbw-fO.DirectorOrdainsHallmark.sh rbev-vessels/rbev-busybox` — hits the heavy rewrite: `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` (conjure jq block, pouch construction, per-platform attest sweep, fact emissions) and the Cloud Build step scripts (`rbgjb/`, `rbgja/`). Watch the Cloud Build console — URL construction failures surface as build-submission errors.
- **Enshrine** a base (if any vessel has `RBRV_IMAGE_ORIGIN` set): exercises `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh` and the anchor-as-path-segment shift.
- **Graft** a local image: `tt/rbw-fO.*` against `rbev-graft-demo` — exercises `rbfd` graft path + `rbgja01-graft-push.sh` if applicable.

After each: confirm artifact layout under `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/` has `image`, `vouch`, `pouch`/none-for-graft, `about`, `attest`, `diags` as sibling basenames — via `tt/rbw-ir.DirectorRekonsImages.sh` (raw GAR tag listing).

### Tier 3 — Hand-run consumer paths

- **Tally**: `tt/rbw-ft.RetrieverTalliesHallmarks.sh` — confirms tally's new basename-enumeration classification works against mixed-shape depot content. Look for the vouched/pending/incomplete counts and per-hallmark listings; any hallmark misclassified surfaces here.
- **Plumb compact + full**: `tt/rbw-fpc.*` and `tt/rbw-fpf.*` on a vouched hallmark — confirms metadata fetch from new `<hallmark>/vouch` and `<hallmark>/about` packages.
- **Summon**: `tt/rbw-fs.RetrieverSummonsHallmark.sh` — confirms retriever pull path against new layout.
- **Charge tadmor**: `tt/rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh` — compose.yml interpolation against new basename shape. Sentry + pentacle + bottle come up cleanly or they don't.
- **Quench tadmor**: `tt/rbw-cQ.Quench.tadmor.sh` — tests cleanup path.

### Tier 4 — Abjure

- **Abjure a freshly-conjured hallmark**: `tt/rbw-fA.DirectorAbjuresHallmark.sh rbev-vessels/rbev-busybox «hallmark»` — exercises the new enumerate-then-delete via GAR REST `/packages` (the biggest algorithmic rewrite in Notch 2b). Confirm all 5-6 basename packages deleted cleanly; confirm `tt/rbw-ir` shows empty for that hallmark.
- **Abjure a pre-AAK hallmark** (if any exist with old-shape content): exercises enumerate-then-delete's topology-agnosticism. Should Just Work because it enumerates the subtree — but worth confirming rather than assuming.

### Triage protocol

On failure:
1. Record the exact tabtarget invocation, stderr, and GAR state (screenshot or `rbw-ir` listing)
2. Classify: **grammar bug** (stale path or tag in bash/Rust), **contract bug** (fact emission mismatch), **integration bug** (new code is right but an adjacent old assumption broke), **AAL-scope** (signature-related, legitimate to defer), or **AAE-scope** (depot-state related, legitimate to defer)
3. Fix grammar and contract bugs inline in this pace — they're part of cutover completion
4. Integration bugs: assess scope; small means fix here, large means spinoff pace with explicit handoff docket
5. AAL/AAE-scope: document and move on; these are out of this pace's contract

### Verification

- Service suite green or triaged
- Crucible suite green or triaged
- One full ordain → summon → charge → quench cycle passes against current depot
- One abjure cycle passes (delete → confirm empty)
- Tally/plumb/rekon reflect new layout correctly on at least one vessel's hallmarks
- No stale-grammar failure modes surface (if they do, they return to AAK for a Notch 5; this pace is consumer-side)

### Out of scope

- Depot regen (`₢A_AAE`) — explicitly gates on this pace completing
- Tabtarget signature simplification (`₢A_AAL`) — orthogonal to path grammar
- Filesystem moorings rename (`₢A_AAJ`) — separate heat
- Any fact-contract harmonization (the `ark_stem` includes-prefix vs. wrest locator excludes-prefix asymmetry flagged in AAK close summary) — surface-level tax, not broken

### References

- `₢A_AAK` — parent migration, 4 notches landed: a9e95201 (1 + 2a), 6c362750 (2b), 93c651f2 (specs), 739b4a35 (Rust catchup)
- `Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` — producer; per-mode fact emission now uniform
- `Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:264–359` — abjure new algorithm
- `Tools/rbk/rbgl_GarLayout.sh` — root constants contract
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs:2210–2414` — four-mode fixture
- `.rbk/rbob_compose.yml:23,57,77` — compose interpolation

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A_
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Three interwoven infrastructure cleanups culminating in a depot regeneration:
6	
7	1. **Resource prefixing** (`₢A_AAA`/`₢A_AAB`/`₢A_AAC`) — introduce `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` (cloud-visible names: GAR, Cloud Build, GCP) and `RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX` (local container and network names). Enables multi-regime isolation on shared infrastructure.
8	
9	2. **GAR categorical layout migration** (`₢A_AAK`/`₢A_AAL`) — reorganize GAR top-level namespace into three prefixed categories. Dissolves the `<vessel>:<hallmark>-<suffix>` hyphen-suffix scheme and the `vouches/` aggregator. Tabtargets taking `(vessel, hallmark)` simplify to `(hallmark)`.
10	
11	3. **Payor credential subdirectory** (`₢A_AAD`) — move `rbro.env` into a `payor/` subdirectory to match the role-subdirectory pattern already established for RBRA credentials.
12	
13	The depot regen at the tail (`₢A_AAE`) is the hard cutover point. All three threads land before it; depot regen validates the new infrastructure in regenerated form.
14	
15	## Design decisions captured
16	
17	### GAR categorical layout
18	
19	Three top-level namespaces under the cloud prefix:
20	
21	```
22	<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/{image, vouch, pouch, about, attest, diags}
23	<prefix>reliquaries/<date>/<tool>
24	<prefix>enshrines/<base>
25	```
26	
27	Each immediate child of `<prefix>hallmarks` IS a hallmark; the six ark types become plain basename siblings. No more vessel-in-path or suffix-on-tag string grammar.
28	
29	### Vessel identity
30	
31	No longer lives in the GAR path. Resolved on demand from the `vouch` or `about` metadata of the hallmark in question. `ls <prefix>hallmarks/` no longer surfaces vessel at a glance; tally and plumb already read metadata for health, and extend the pattern where operation prose needs the vessel name.
32	
33	### Attest vs vouch
34	
35	Both persist as peer basenames under `<hallmark>/`. Attestations advertise during build for SLSA provenance; vouches check them at consumption. Load-bearing split — retained by design.
36	
37	### Pouch
38	
39	A `FROM scratch` OCI image carrying build context (Dockerfile + supporting files) to Cloud Build's first step. Present for conjure/bind, absent for graft (graft is local push — no GCB involved). Now lives at `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/pouch` in the new layout.
40	
41	### SUFFIX → BASENAME constant rename
42	
43	`RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_*` constants persist but rename to `RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_*`; their values drop the leading hyphen. Role shifts from "suffix to hyphen-concat" to "literal basename of a sibling file." The concat grammar disappears entirely from ark path construction.
44	
45	### `vouches/` aggregator removal
46	
47	`RBGC_VOUCHES_PACKAGE` existed to enable hallmark-only vouch lookup without knowing the vessel. The new layout delivers the same property naturally — every hallmark has its own `vouch` basename under `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/`. The aggregator becomes redundant and is deleted.
48	
49	### Cutover discipline
50	
51	Hard cutover — old and new path shapes do not coexist in live code. Depot regen at `₢A_AAE` is the forcing function; all layout work lands before it.
52	
53	## References
54	
55	- `₢A_AAJ` — filesystem-side config-directory naming research (orthogonal to this heat). Decisions recorded in that pace: `rbmm_moorings` umbrella, `rbm*_` prefix family, hard-cutover at depot regen. Filesystem rename itself flows to a separate follow-up heat and is out of this heat's scope.
56	- `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh:122-131` — current ark-suffix constants and `RBGC_VOUCHES_PACKAGE`; this heat renames to `_BASENAME_*` and removes the aggregator.
57	- `Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:712-733` — pouch definition and current concat-based path construction.
58	- `Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:440-650` — current per-suffix HEAD/DELETE dance; collapses to subtree operations in `₢A_AAK`.
59
